frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: We should teach boys from a young age to see every woman as someone’s mother, daughter or sister. The day society remembers that, respect will come automatically. A woman’s true value is in the care and sacrifice she brings to her family, and that is why she must always be honoured.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames women’s worth and entitlement to respect primarily through family roles and service to others, rather than as individuals. It encodes relational framing ('mother, daughter or sister') and sacrifice glorification as the basis of respect.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
